title: Canon Adjudications
description: The persisted artifact canon_adjudication_audit.py reads (default path {durable_root}/canon_adjudications.json) and cross-checks against canon.json's freshly recomputed required-item worklist -- NEVER writes verdicts or risk-acceptances itself (--init only ever writes the empty template below). Authored by a human reviewer or a schema-validated codex workflow. 'adjudications' covers categories 1-3 (duplicate_source_form/existing_merge/candidate_missed_merge_pair) and category 5 (homonym_split, enumerated only when canon_senses.json is non-empty), keyed by '{kind}::' + sha256(canonical_json(identity)); each record needs a valid verdict_class plus non-empty reviewed_by/reason. 'degenerate_cap_overrides' covers category 3's above-cap risk-acceptance, keyed by the single project-global scope token '__canon__'; each record must carry the FRESH cap-identity (entity_count/pair_count/cap/entity_set_fingerprint) canon_adjudication_audit.py's --check prints, or it is reported stale. 'review_queue_risk_overrides' covers category 4 (review_queue_unresolved items that are risk-accepted rather than drained), keyed the same way as their matching required item. Category 5's own collapsed_split/evidence_unverified findings are NEVER satisfied by any adjudication record here -- they reconcile/verify against canon.json/manifest.json directly and are always-blocking. | VALIDATION LAYERS: this schema is the full AUTHORING contract -- validate the whole file against it at authoring time (a human reviewer or schema-validated codex workflow). The runtime reader (canon_adjudication_audit.py) enforces the load-bearing fields of CURRENTLY-REQUIRED items plus structural readability; it intentionally does NOT re-validate the content of ORPHAN records (adjudication/override keys that match no currently-required item) -- orphans are reported for pruning but are non-blocking, because an orphan cannot hide a current risk (required items are recomputed fresh from canon.json). Full-file schema conformance beyond the load-bearing current-item fields is the authoring layer's responsibility, deliberately not duplicated in the stdlib-only reader.

_contract.description: Free-text pointer to canon_adjudication_audit.py's own module docstring for the full contract -- documentation only, not load-bearing; not read or validated by the reader, so left unconstrained to match the reader.

adjudications.description: Keyed by required-item key ('duplicate_source_form::<hash>' / 'existing_merge::<hash>' / 'candidate_missed_merge_pair::<hash>'). Each record's own 'kind'/identity fields are for human readability and orphan-detection only -- the KEY is authoritative for matching against the currently-required item.

kind.description: Advisory human-readability label (normally duplicate_source_form / existing_merge / candidate_missed_merge_pair / homonym_split). canon_adjudication_audit.py does NOT read or validate this field -- the record KEY is authoritative for matching against the currently-required item. Left unconstrained so this schema and the reader agree: a mistyped advisory label must never block the gate.

reviewed_by.description: Non-empty and not whitespace-only: human name or codex-workflow id. A missing/empty/blank value never satisfies the gate.

timestamp.description: Advisory; not read or validated by canon_adjudication_audit.py, so left unconstrained to match the reader.

degenerate_cap_overrides.description: At most one meaningful key, '__canon__' -- canon is project-global, so there is exactly one cap scope.

timestamp.description: Advisory; not read or validated by canon_adjudication_audit.py, so left unconstrained to match the reader.

entity_count.description: Must match the fresh cap-identity canon_adjudication_audit.py's --check prints, or this override is reported stale.

entity_set_fingerprint.description: sha256(canonical_json(sorted distinct normalized canonical_target_form over proper-name entries)). Any change to the entity set (add/remove/rename) changes this and stales the override.

review_queue_risk_overrides.description: Keyed by 'review_queue_unresolved::<hash>', the same key construction as the matching review_queue_unresolved required item (hash of the whole queued item's own canonical JSON -- any content change re-blocks under a new key).

timestamp.description: Advisory; not read or validated by canon_adjudication_audit.py, so left unconstrained to match the reader.
